mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive 20-day returns occur in less than 50% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 65% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts amplifies aggregate demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions higher and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average indicates supply-side contraction that suppresses corporate revenue growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 60% of periods where INDPRO trades below its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with a tightening labor market forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL month-over-month change > 0.3% and PAYEMS month-over-month change > 100000
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in more than 58% of months where both CPIAUCSL and PAYEMS exceed their respective thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with stronger corporate profit expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as markets price in improved earnings trajectories.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative over 5-year out-of-sample periods or positive 5-day returns occur in less than 45% of high-GDP quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50000 jobs in a single month signals immediate economic weakness that triggers algorithmic selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 62% of months following a payroll decline exceeding 50000.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial output alongside declining federal funds rates creates a favorable supply-demand balance that boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Combined signal of rising INDPRO and falling FEDFUNDS produces negative 60-day returns in more than 57% of observations.
